summary.overview:
- **Client Needs:** Client has 350 employees, seeking a low-touch engagement survey system with regular feedback.  

- **Launch Timeline:** Engagement survey to go live by end of May; pricing and platform details needed urgently.  

- **Culture Monkey Overview:** Agile platform with customizable surveys, 10 million benchmarking data points, and anonymous feedback by default.  

- **Advanced Features:** Supports multilingual surveys, real-time heat maps, and integrates with HR systems for continuous feedback.  

- **Pricing Structure:** Annual cost is €10,500 for 350 employees, covering unlimited services, with a price lock until 2029.  

- **Fast Implementation:** Launch typically in under five weeks, possibly accelerated to three weeks to meet client deadline.

Include a direct calendar link in the email for Sarah and her colleagues to book follow-up meetings (26:08)
summary.notes:
## **Client Background and Needs**

This section clarifies the client’s specific engagement survey needs and timeline.

- **Transition from Previous Vendor with Specific Needs** (00:23)  
    - Sarah explained the company has **350 employees** and previously used **Lattice** but moved away due to usability and unclear focus.  
    - They want a **low-touch system** with a **bank of varied pulse survey questions** sent every two weeks or monthly.  
    - The goal is to track feedback continuously across engagement, wellness, workflow, and leadership.  
    - They are evaluating multiple vendors, including **Work Leap**, but have concerns about fit and timeline.

- **Urgent Engagement Survey Launch Target** (01:56)  
    - The client wants to go live with an engagement survey by **end of May**, creating a **tight deadline**.  
    - Sarah emphasized the need to understand pricing and platform capabilities quickly to meet this timeline.  
    - They are open to a solution that balances speed, ease of use, and comprehensive question frameworks.

## **Culture Monkey Platform Overview and Capabilities**

This section covers how Culture Monkey fits the client’s needs with flexibility and depth.

- **Agile Platform with Extensive Question Templates and People Science Support** (03:50)  
    - Sunil positioned Culture Monkey as a **lightweight, agile platform** with templates for pulse, engagement, and other surveys.  
    - The platform supports **custom questions** and offers dedicated **people science team** support for tailored question frameworks.  
    - Their **benchmarking data includes over 10 million data points**, ensuring scientifically backed survey design.  
    - Surveys are **anonymous by default**, enhancing honest feedback from employees.

- **Seamless Integration with Bamboo HR and Slack** (05:50)  
    - Culture Monkey integrates directly with **Bamboo HR** for automatic employee data syncing.  
    - Native **Slack integration** allows survey deployment via Slack, along with other channels like WhatsApp, email, QR codes, and Microsoft Teams.  
    - Integration supports **automated survey lifecycles** including onboarding, exit, and milestone-based surveys for continuous listening.

- **Real-Time Analytics and Custom Reporting** (07:23)  
    - Platform dashboards provide **real-time insight the moment surveys launch** with live participation tracking.  
    - Reports can be customized for CEO-level presentations, delivered as PDFs or other formats.  
    - Manager and team-level views are available with strict data access control.  
    - The AI-powered chatbot offers **instant data queries and action recommendations** for HR and managers.

## **Advanced Survey Features and Reporting**

This section highlights the platform’s unique survey design and analytics capabilities.

- **Flexible Survey Design with Dynamic and Multilingual Questions** (09:22)  
    - Surveys can be anonymous or non-anonymous, with **dynamic questions that adapt based on prior answers or demographics**.  
    - Multilingual support covers **130 languages** with near-instant translations for future scaling.  
    - QR-only surveys enable quick anonymous feedback in physical office spaces.

- **Detailed Heat Maps and Drill-Down Analytics by Demographics** (12:46)  
    - Real-time heat maps reflect the entire org structure pulled from Bamboo HR, breaking data by teams, locations, managers, and subteams.  
    - Managers can see engagement scores, NPS, and driver scores filtered by their direct reports or entire hierarchy.  
    - The system highlights problem areas instantly, enabling targeted intervention.

- **Benchmarking and Longitudinal Insights Embedded in Platform** (13:50)  
    - Sarah’s query on benchmarking was confirmed: **benchmark data is integrated into dashboards by industry, region, and company size**.  
    - The platform compares current survey results to previous surveys, showing areas of improvement or decline automatically.  
    - Question-level reporting allows detailed analysis and side-by-side demographic comparisons.

## **Action Management and Manager Empowerment**

This section covers how Culture Monkey supports follow-up and management accountability.

- **AI-Driven Action Recommendations and Kanban Tracking** (17:29)  
    - The platform’s **Ask Cooper chatbot** provides instant, tailored action recommendations based on survey data.  
    - A built-in Kanban board tracks action progress, assigns tasks to managers, and supports two-way communication for coaching.  
    - Managers can create their own actions or receive assignments from HR, enhancing accountability.

- **Manager Dashboards with Controlled Data Access** (18:42)  
    - Managers with **5+ direct reports get dashboard access** showing engagement metrics for their team and entire reporting hierarchy.  
    - Dashboards include internal and external benchmarking to help managers understand performance context.  
    - Data visibility is strictly controlled; chatbot insights are filtered based on manager permissions.  
    - This empowers managers to take timely, data-driven actions.

## **Lifecycle Surveys and Custom Attribute Integration**

This section discusses comprehensive employee lifecycle survey automation and data enrichment.

- **Automated Surveys from Onboarding to Exit** (21:16)  
    - Culture Monkey automates surveys across the full employee lifecycle: onboarding, milestones, promotions, and exit.  
    - Exit survey analytics spotlight teams or managers with low scores and identify specific negative feedback hotspots.  
    - This supports proactive retention and engagement strategies.

- **Integration of Custom Employee Attributes for Deeper Insights** (22:34)  
    - Sarah asked if internal employee ratings can be connected; Sunil confirmed **custom attributes like performance ratings can be imported**.  
    - This enables cross-analysis, such as correlating exit feedback with performance ratings or onboarding scores.  
    - The platform can generate custom tabs and heat maps based on these attributes to uncover deeper patterns.

## **Implementation Timeline and Pricing**

This section defines the deployment schedule and cost with clear commitments.

- **Fast Implementation Timeline Aligned with Client Needs** (22:58)  
    - Culture Monkey typically launches surveys in **under five weeks**, with a possibility to accelerate to **about three weeks** to meet the client’s May deadline.  
    - Dedicated teams handle setup, survey testing, lifecycle configuration, and training for smooth onboarding.

- **All-Inclusive Annual Pricing with Stability Guarantee** (24:13)  
    - The annual subscription price is **€10,500 for 350 employees**, covering unlimited surveys, users, integrations, and people science.  
    - No extra fees for setup or implementation and **price lock guaranteed through 2029** to avoid surprise renewals.  
    - Pricing supports long-term partnership and predictable budgeting.

- **Sandbox Access and Follow-Up Support** (25:07)  
    - Sunil offered a **48-hour sandbox access** for hands-on evaluation, pending internal compliance approvals.  
    - He committed to sending a detailed proposal with pricing and calendar links for easy follow-up.  
    - Sarah appreciated the demo and plans to review and come back with questions, showing strong interest.

summary.notes:
## **Client Needs and Organizational Overview**

The meeting began with John Harpley explaining his recent CEO role starting August 2024 and the structure of his organization across multiple locations totaling about **92 employees** (01:23).  
- John detailed seven distinct teams including a **17-person building construction team** on Groot Island and three off-island controlled entities with staff counts of **14, 10, and 24 respectively**.  
- He emphasized a cultural shift from a toxic environment to a more positive, engaged workforce on Groot Island, but flagged leadership trust issues in Darwin after recent terminations.  
- John’s primary goal is to gather actionable feedback from all teams to identify leadership skill gaps and improve trust and outcomes, linking this to additional leadership training programs he is exploring.  

Vish acknowledged these challenges and positioned Culture Monkey as a solution specifically designed to collect and analyze employee feedback, helping John focus on next steps to rebuild trust and leadership confidence (03:48).  

## **Culture Monkey Platform Capabilities**

Vish presented Culture Monkey as a comprehensive employee engagement tool used by **300+ customers across 100 countries** to bridge employee feedback and action (06:10).  
- The platform offers continuous listening via annual, biannual, or pulse surveys across multiple channels including email, SMS, WhatsApp, Teams, Slack, and QR codes to maximize employee participation regardless of location or language.  
- It features a rich survey template library with customization options and expert support for question framing, ensuring relevant and clear feedback collection.  
- A key differentiator is the advanced analytics layer providing real-time heat maps showing performance drivers by team and demographics, enabling detailed insights at granular levels like location or reporting manager (08:20).  
- The system includes an AI-powered chatbot, Ask Cooper, that instantly generates action plans and insights without needing third-party consultants, streamlining decision-making post-survey (15:45).  

The platform also supports collaborative action planning with customizable tasks assigned to managers or HR, including comments and progress tracking, designed to help leaders address issues identified from survey data (17:07).  

## **Manager and Reporting Features**

Vish demonstrated the manager dashboard designed for leaders with more than **five direct reports**, giving them real-time views of engagement scores, participation rates, and ENPS metrics along with strengths and weaknesses at the driver level (18:08).  
- The dashboard provides both internal and external benchmarking, allowing managers to compare performance against industry standards.  
- Historical comparisons across multiple surveys enable tracking progress and identifying trends over time, supporting continuous improvement and accountability (19:27).  
- Ask Cooper can be leveraged here to quickly analyze changes and recommend next best steps, keeping managers focused on what matters most.  

This approach supports John's goal of improving leadership trust by giving managers clear, actionable insights and tools to respond effectively to employee feedback.  

## **Pricing and Decision Considerations**

John expressed a need to justify spending due to his organization being an indigenous non-profit investing **$1.8 million annually** in community programs, requiring value assurance before board approval (20:20).  
- Vish offered a **non-profit discounted price of AUD 6,500 annually** for up to 100 employees, inclusive of all features and unlimited surveys, with a typical implementation timeline of under three weeks (21:24).  
- John clarified GST inclusion concerns, requiring confirmation whether the price includes GST or if an additional **AUD 650** applies, which Vish committed to confirm promptly (22:41).  
- Payment flexibility was also noted, with options for bank transfer, credit card, or Stripe to ease procurement.  

John plans to present the Culture Monkey offer alongside two other vendors to his board on Monday, positioning the price as mid-range but needing board approval before proceeding (22:03).  

## **Next Steps and Client Engagement**

The meeting closed with Vish committing to send detailed pricing and platform information via email and offering ongoing support to answer further questions or provide demos (23:37).  
- Vish emphasized their dedicated account manager will assist with survey scheduling, testing, and launch to minimize client workload, covering **95% of implementation tasks** except for employee data provision (10:32).  
- John was invited to reach out anytime for additional support, showing openness to continued dialogue and partnership.  

This sets a clear path for John to evaluate Culture Monkey internally with full vendor transparency and a strong support framework to facilitate adoption if approved.

summary.overview:
- **Culture Monkey supports 80 employees with customizable, multilingual surveys and real-time analytics.** Surveys include diverse distribution options like Slack and QR codes.  
- **Managers get engagement scores for their teams without seeing individual responses.** Summary scores and weaknesses help managers improve team dynamics.  
- **Automated action plans are generated based on survey feedback.** These plans can be edited and assigned to managers for accountability.  
- **Employee data upload requires detailed attributes for role assignment.** Essential fields include name, gender, email, and manager information.  
- **Annual subscription for 85 employees costs US$3,750, with some room for negotiation.** Only annual contracts are available, affecting selection among vendors.

summary.notes:
## **Platform Overview and Survey Capabilities**

The meeting clarified how Culture Monkey’s platform supports employee engagement surveys with robust customization, multilingual options, and real-time analytics.

- **Culture Monkey’s end-to-end engagement platform suits 80 employees with multilingual survey and real-time insights** (01:54)  
    - The platform supports annual, quarterly, and ad hoc surveys with a library of research-backed templates.  
    - Surveys can be sent via Slack, WhatsApp, MS Teams, QR codes, or text messages.  
    - Real-time dashboards display participation rates and feedback heat maps as surveys progress.  
    - A dedicated support team helps with survey framing, employee data upload, and ongoing account management.

- **Survey creation is flexible with full customization and multilingual support** (06:26)  
    - Users select or customize templates and question types including rating, yes/no, multiple choice, and open text.  
    - Surveys can be anonymous or confidential, with the ability to add custom questions like eNPS.  
    - Employee audience is uploaded via Excel, including info like name, team, manager, location, and join date.  
    - Survey launch and close dates are set in advance, with pre-launch employee notifications possible.

- **Platform ensures secure, unique survey links and multi-device accessibility** (24:21)  
    - Each participant receives a unique survey link with authentication to prevent sharing.  
    - Surveys can be completed on laptops, smartphones, or tablets via any browser.  
    - Partial survey responses are saved so employees can resume later before final submission.  
    - Once submitted, responses cannot be changed, ensuring data integrity during the open survey period.

- **Culture Monkey integrates a chatbot for easy data querying and action planning** (20:51)  
    - The inbuilt chatbot “Ask Cooper” functions like ChatGPT to answer questions about survey scores or suggest action plans.  
    - This reduces manual effort in reporting and helps managers quickly understand team feedback and next steps.  
    - The chatbot operates within the platform environment, accessible via simple prompts.

## **Manager Role and Reporting Structure**

The platform clearly separates manager survey participation from their reporting access, balancing anonymity with actionable insights.

- **Manager engagement scores are calculated for each manager’s direct team** (05:33)  
    - Surveys can be targeted to specific managers and their departments.  
    - Managers receive a dashboard showing engagement scores, eNPS, strengths, and weaknesses at a driver level.  
    - This allows managers to focus on their team’s feedback without seeing individual responses.

- **Anonymous survey responses remain anonymous even when managers access aggregated reports** (15:16)  
    - Managers only see summary scores and driver-level insights, not individual feedback or respondent identities.  
    - The system restricts data visibility to protect anonymity while enabling meaningful action.  
    - Access rights for managers to view reports are controlled by super admins from the backend.

- **Action plans are automatically generated based on survey feedback and can be assigned to managers** (13:50)  
    - The platform suggests actions for low-scoring drivers like work environment or autonomy.  
    - Action items can be edited, assigned to specific managers, and collaborated on with other admins.  
    - This supports accountability and follow-through on survey outcomes at the team level.

- **Role-based access separates survey participation from reporting and action management** (17:42)  
    - Managers receive survey links to complete the survey anonymously without seeing reports initially.  
    - After survey completion and data processing, managers get access to their dashboard for insights and action planning.  
    - Employee data upload includes manager assignments, enabling role-based report distribution automatically.

## **Data Preparation and Survey Security**

The discussion addressed data formatting, participant information requirements, and security features to maintain data integrity.

- **Employee data upload requires detailed attributes for analytics and role assignment** (19:59)  
    - Required fields include first name, gender, employee ID, email, date of joining, team, location, and manager.  
    - Additional custom attributes can be added to improve analytics granularity and filtering.  
    - Manager information is essential for assigning reports and dashboards correctly.

- **Unique survey links with authentication prevent unauthorized access or multiple completions** (24:21)  
    - Each link is tied to an individual’s email and protected by OTP or email verification.  
    - Forwarded links cannot be used by others, ensuring response validity and privacy.  
    - The system tracks completion status, allowing edits only before final submission.

- **Survey responses can be saved mid-completion and resumed later within the open period** (23:16)  
    - Partial answers are stored so employees can finish surveys over multiple sessions.  
    - Survey open and close dates are configurable, controlling participation windows.  
    - This improves completion rates and user convenience without risking data loss.

- **Surveys are fully accessible on all devices, enhancing employee participation ease** (25:09)  
    - Compatible with smartphones, tablets, and desktops via any modern browser.  
    - Mobile-friendly design supports employees working remotely or in the field.

## **Pricing and Contract Terms**

The meeting concluded with pricing details and contract constraints relevant to the company’s budget and timeline.

- **Annual subscription pricing for 85 employees is US$3,750 with a slight negotiable discount** (26:55)  
    - This rate includes unlimited surveys, a dedicated customer success team, and chatbot access.  
    - The per-user cost is approximately **US$44** per year.  
    - The lowest offered price was **US$3,500**, with no further discounts possible.

- **No short-term or project-based pricing options are available, only annual contracts** (28:12)  
    - The client requested shorter-term options for 2–3 months, but the vendor confirmed only yearly terms.  
    - This may affect vendor selection based on company budget and project duration needs.

- **The client is evaluating multiple vendors, weighing support capabilities against cost** (29:11)  
    - Pricing and feature fit will guide the final decision for the employee engagement platform.  
    - The vendor offered to provide additional demos for other stakeholders if needed.  
    - Follow-up communication will share detailed pricing and platform information for further consideration.
